title: ConsumeResponse
description: Response from POST /v2/permits/:id/consume. The consume endpoint returns a pointer to the newly-issued proof rather than the full proof object; callers fetch the object via GET /v2/proofs/:id when they need the signed envelope. Rationale: consume is on the hot path of every protect() call, so the response stays tiny.

proof_id.description: UUID of the proof row. Fetch with GET /v2/proofs/:id to get the full signed envelope.

execution_status.description: Echo of the execution_status submitted. Present so callers can assert the server recorded what they sent.

audit_hash.description: Audit-chain hash of this proof's audit_events row. Same meaning as Proof.audit_hash. Returned here so callers can correlate with audit exports without a second round-trip.
